candidate_problems.text: How can 'integrated counterfactual dependence' or 'load-bearing representational role' be defined and measured non-circularly, so that it distinguishes conscious from merely complex or highly interconnected information-processing systems?

Why should a dense web of mutual constraint, control, error-correction, and self-prediction generate phenomenal consciousness at all, rather than only sophisticated functional organization without any felt aspect?

What are the necessary boundaries of the relevant system: which components must belong to the mutually constraining model-maintaining architecture for consciousness to arise, and how do modularity, external scaffolding, or distributed embodiment affect the attribution of consciousness?
